notes: Bishop addressed by Pope Gregory the Great around 594 AD regarding church discipline.

docs.title: Letter 4042
docs.author: Gregory the Great (Wisigothic)
docs.subject_summary: PL77 Book 4, Ep 42: AD SEBASTIANUM EPISCOPUM.
docs.excerpt: The matter before you requires the kind of episcopal firmness that is sometimes harder to maintain than any other virtue. To be clear and direct when clarity and directness are uncomfortable, to insist on proper procedure when those who benefit from confusion prefer otherwise — this is the

docs.title: New History, Book 4
docs.author: Zosimus
docs.excerpt: … Valens, knowing his ability both in civil and military affairs, appointed him to the command of his army, and entrusted him with the whole management of the war.
